memory.summary: The scholarship's solo nature and long-term payoff feel safer than the immediate fun, yet my stress makes the required focus hours daunting.

Starting this now creates slack for friction, proving the scholarship is an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a crisis. Since the fun option isn't time-locked, I can invest early to lower Thursday stress without losing anything.
My progress feels painfully slow despite the hours spent, and the looming deadline still creates a heavy weight in my mind.

I moved the scholarship forward, yet my low energy and lingering stress make that progress feel heavy. I let the casual fun pass by, hoping early work will ease future friction without forcing myself to push harder now.

The progress feels disproportionately heavy compared to the time spent, yet moving it forward seems necessary to protect my energy for socializing later. I'm hoping this early friction will eventually vanish so I can just be present with friends.
I'm relieved to have moved the scholarship forward, but the progress still feels heavy and slow. I need to keep going just enough to protect my energy for time with friends later.
The progress feels heavy despite the time spent, yet I'm relieved to have moved it forward enough to protect my energy for friends later.
Another small step forward, yet the friction still feels disproportionate to my energy. I hope this work will soon lift enough so I can truly be present with my friends later.
That 1.5-hour push was exhausting, but seeing the progress finally move feels like a small victory to preserve my energy for friends later.
The tiny 0.02 progress gain feels heavy for the effort spent, yet I'm relieved to have cleared just enough friction to protect my energy for friends later.
That final 0.05 progress feels earned but costly, leaving me drained yet relieved enough to finally look forward to my friends without the weight of unfinished tasks.
The 0.96 completion feels like a small victory that finally clears my mind enough to cherish time with friends, even though I'm completely drained.

desires: To finish the scholarship essay without another late-night push so I don't feel like a burden to my close friend group tomorrow.
To be able to sit with my friends and actually listen to them without my mind being stuck on unfinished tasks or feeling guilty about my energy levels.
To avoid any friction or awkwardness when I inevitably have to ask for help or explain my absence due to exhaustion.

goals.description: Complete the final ~4% of the scholarship essay and upload files to 'lock in' my safe status, ensuring I can attend the cohort_3 dinner tonight without carrying academic dread or feeling unreliable.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that my time with cohort_3 is non-negotiable; entering that space stressed would damage the trust we've built. While I dislike pushing through severe exhaustion (avoidant conflict), my deliberate planning horizon tells me this is a low-cost investment now to guarantee friction-free social interaction later. I will finish just enough to secure the outcome without burning out completely.

goals.rationale: I need to monitor my physical stamina (currently 6/10). If writing drains me too much, I'll have nothing left for the most important social event of the day. It's better to submit a slightly shorter draft than to burn out before the meal.

desires: To quietly finish the scholarship essay tonight so I don't have to explain my absence or stress about missing the deadline tomorrow, even though it feels safer to just play a game now.
